    Mobile search

      • Creating a mentally healthy nation for all

        The APA Foundation promotes mental health literacy, connects underserved communities to needed care, and shifts attitudes about mental health.

        Learn More

      • Mental Health Care Works

      • APA Foundation Fellowships

      • About the Foundation

        82K

        School staff members have been trained with our Notice. Talk. Act® at School framework

        Learn more

      Learn more about our work where you live, learn, work, worship and play.

      We administer programs, fellowship training, and grants nationwide to provide resources to communities that spread the message that mental health care works.

      • Schools

      • Justice

      • Workplace

      • Faith

      • Play

      • Fellowships
      Learn More About Our Programs

      Latest from the Foundation

      Discover the APA Foundation's latest, offering insights into groundbreaking strides in mental health support and awareness.

      • Feb 18, 2026

        APA Foundation and Hispanic Access Foundation Announce New Collaboration

      • Jan 29, 2026

        Grant from Mother Cabrini Health Foundation Enhances APA Foundation's Work to Provide Serious Mental Illness Education and Resources in the Bronx and Beyond

      • Nov 24, 2025

        APA Foundation’s Signature Campaign Launches New Resources During National Family Caregiver Month

      Use Next and Previous buttons to navigate, or jump to a slide using the slide dots.

      "The APA Foundation Public Psychiatry Fellowship has been one of the best experiences of my psychiatry training. The program exposes me to mental health providers across the country and the world. I am a better leader, clinician, and advocate thanks to this program."

      Jonas Attilus, M.D., M.P.H.

      APAF Public Psychiatry Fellow Class 2024

      "After the [Notice. Talk. Act. at School] training, the staff are so much more comfortable and so much more willing to engage students. This training is not just one more to stick on the shelf."

      Natalie Lee

      Special Education Counselor, Titus County, TX

      “Association House [of Chicago] is honored to be recognized for our commitment to prioritizing the mental health and addressing challenges faced by minority communities. This [APA Foundation] grant will go toward our relentless effort to continue breaking down barriers and providing vital support to those who need it most.”

      Juan Carlos Linares

      President and CEO, Association House of Chicago
